title: 0.00000000000000000 Tarumi and Shimamura
description: Tarumi reflects on her childhood friendship with Shimamura—how they grew apart, and the lingering regret that follows. A bittersweet look at the distance between people.
content:
Do you like clay?

Huh?

Like, the clay you mush and squish.

I–I guess? Oh, but you know, I don't like when it gets under my nails.

You should keep your nails nice and trim.

Wh–who are you supposed to be?

Shima-chan, of course.

Wha–?

Now, come along and play with Shima-chan.

……Shima-chan?

Would it be weird to say Shimamura-chan?

Everyone calls me Shima-chan, ya know.

Oh-hooh.

Hmph. Hmm, but I'm gonna call you something else.

Your first name's Hougetsu, right?

Yuppers.

Okay, then… Hou-chan?

That sounds kinda stretched out.

Well, you're always spaced out, so it works, no?

No, it doesn't.

Well, I guess I'll leave it at Shima-chan.

See that you do.

Why do I feel like you're talking down to me……?

Fine, then I'll call you Taru-chan!

Kay!

You're kinda weird, aren't ya, Shima-chan?

You don't say?

Yeah, you talk funny, for one.

Well, you're funny-looking.

Boooo.

My mom talks like this, so it's totally normal for me.

Huuuh, your mom? She's probably super Shima-chan-ish.

Yeah, she's almost all Shima-chan.

She is, eh?

But I'm the only one who's totally Shima-chan from my head to my toes, you know.

Heh-heh, not if I'm squeezing your hand like this. Then a little bit of me gets mixed into you.

Aaagh! You ruined my perfect Shima-chan!

Shima-chan's melting!

Shima-chan, which do you like better, dogs or cats?

Heh-heh-heh. I'll act it out, so try to guess. Here I gooo! Maah!

Huh?... Is that a cat?

Maah, maah.

…Whassat?

Shima-chan, bad news! The aliens are attacking!

What. Did. You. Say?

That's what they said on TV.

Oh, I saw that yesterday eve myself.

Ah, thought so. Pretty cool, right?

Mm-hmm. Quite the display.

What do you think the aliens want?

Hmm… Some candy maybe?

You mean, that's what Interstellar Shima-chan wants.

She'll settle for an apple, too.

I'll never give in to the aliens. I'll fight to the end!

How dare you! I'm gonna getcha! I'm gonna getcha!

Aah-ha-ha!

Hey, uh, do you have any other friends, Shima-chan?

Hmm? I'm friends with everyone.

I see.

You're my pal, Taru-chan.

I already knew thaaat.

You did, did you?

I don't have a lot of friends, so I'm kinda jealous of you.

But, Tarumi, my friends are your friends, you know?

That's not how it works.

Why nooot?

That might be a bit too complicated for you to understand, Shima-chan…

What is this behavior? Stop trying to act so big.

Heh-heh-heh, I am a little taller than you, Shima-chan.

But I'm gonna outgrow you next year.

Nuh-uh, 'cos I'm gonna grow even more.

Fine, then how about we both grow together?

Okay… That's what I love about you, Shima-chan.

Don't you just? Don't you just? Ha-ha-ha!

……You knew?

Shima-chan, what class are you?

Shima-chan, wait up!

Hey, Shima-chan…

Shima-chan!

Memories floated to the surface of my mind as I let the sounds of class wash over me. The recollections rose like bubbles and hit the ceiling without popping, never disappearing—growing and multiplying, threatening to suffocate me. The blackboard I had been consciously avoiding was now completely covered in white, so thick I couldn't see past it even if I tried.

'Just give up already,' I whispered to myself, softly, so nobody else could hear.

Class had finished, and I'd started packing my things to go home when my mother called. I knew she would.

'Okay, I'll stop by on my way home. It's fine; I'll get some other things while I'm at it.'

My mother always decided which pit stops I'd make after school. I was in charge of about half the housework. But that was just the way our family worked; I wasn't unsatisfied or anything. Besides, even if I had a lot of free time on my hands, I wouldn't have too much to do with it.

Sometimes, I heard news about that girl through my mom's connections. I'd learned she hadn't gotten along with her parents in middle school, which surprised me—she hadn't seemed like the type. The girl I knew was cheerful and could wrap you up with her smile. That was just the kind of girl she was.

I'd been thinking about her a lot lately. Did that mean I wanted to see her again?

I didn't have any friends I felt close enough to rely on, so maybe my heart had started leaning that way. If I wanted to see her, I could easily do it. We both knew where the other lived. But she was so far away. Emotionally, she was miles and miles from me.

Which one of us had left the other first? If one of us took the first step, would the other follow and bring us closer?

I'd only heard stories about her, so I didn't know what she looked like anymore. If I passed her on the street, would I recognize her? Would she…? No, definitely not. She might not even remember me at all.

The girl I knew was soft, super-bouncy, and flew every which way. In the past, I would hold her hand and get to fly with her. But I let go of that hand once, and she never came back. I didn't chase after her, either.

Once I got to middle school, everyone around me and I got so busy and overwhelmed that I lost sight of her. Then, a little while later, pricks of regret hit me for letting that happen. That regret would at times thin out, and at others, rush over me like violent waves that filled my head to the brim—just like now.

Back when we were close, I ran into her in town so many times. But strangely enough, once we grew apart, I never came across her again. If your heart or state of mind could naturally shape how close you were with someone—or determine who you met—I think I'd be set. It wasn't just her; there were plenty of other friends I'd lost touch with along the way.

But she was the only one I ever thought about. She must've been special, in so many ways. I was sure of it.

I searched the sky for the moon that had not yet risen and thought, Where is that special one floating now? Then I left school behind and headed first to the butchery. I could've just as easily gone straight to the supermarket, but I decided on this order on the spot. That was all, I swear. It was just a feeling, a mood.

……If your heart or state of mind could naturally shape how close you were with someone—or determine who you met—I was set. At least, that's how I took it.

And then, just like looking up to see that the moon had silently risen in the blue sky, I found something unexpected.

I found that girl.

Together with Shima-chan.
